notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 3 Los Altos locations totaling 18 courts: McKenzie Park (6), Los Altos Golf and Country Club (4), Egan Junior High School (8). The city's own official parks & recreation site (losaltosca.gov) was used as the primary source per this project's standard, and independently confirms McKenzie Park (city-run outdoor, shared with tennis) and Egan Gym (membership indoor pickleball at Egan Junior High School) as currently operating. Los Altos Golf and Country Club is independently confirmed by its own official site (lagcc.com), which explicitly states 'four pickleball courts.' Historical note for human reviewers: Los Altos ran an earlier (2021-2023) outdoor pilot across FOUR parks — Marymeade (2 courts), McKenzie (4 courts), Montclaire (2 courts), and Rosita (6 courts) — but the city's current pickleball FAQ explicitly states 'All courts at Marymeade, Montclaire, and Rosita Parks remain unchanged' under the modified program that took effect May 1, 2023, which instead expanded McKenzie to 6 dedicated courts. So Marymeade, Montclaire, and Rosita are NOT current pickleball venues and are correctly omitted here — they're tennis-only today. See mckenzie-park's notes for sourcing. Egan Junior High School has a genuine, unresolved multi-source conflict on court count and indoor/outdoor structure (see its notes); confirmed left false there pending human review. Could not confirm a numeric Google Maps cid for McKenzie Park or Egan Junior High School despite repeated attempts (resolved short-links, geocoding directories, and direct Maps place-URL fetches) — both left null per this project's convention of never falling back to a place_id or /data= URL. A numeric cid was successfully confirmed for Los Altos Golf and Country Club via its own site's Google Maps short link.

venues.name: McKenzie Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (asphalt); permanent pickleball lines painted on 2 existing tennis courts (Courts #1 and #2) — not standalone dedicated pickleball courts
venues.netType: Portable nets, provided by the city (players bring their own paddles and balls)
venues.price: Free (drop-in, during pickleball priority hours); a separate court-reservation fee of $10/hr resident, $8/hr nonprofit applies to the city's general tennis-court reservation system — unclear whether/when it applies to pickleball specifically, see notes
venues.hours: Pickleball priority Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat, 6am-10pm; Tennis priority Tue/Thu/Sun, 6am-10pm (shared courts; city-sponsored tennis lessons get priority over both). One secondary source instead states general park hours of 6am-9pm daily — not reconciled, official page's 6am-10pm pickleball-specific hours preferred here.
venues.weather: Mid-Peninsula foothill town at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains — milder and more sheltered than inland San Jose/Santa Clara, with warm dry summers and mild wet winters; well suited to year-round outdoor play.
venues.notes: Core facts (6 courts, outdoor, city-run, overlaid on 2 tennis courts, free) are unanimous across the official city site, Pickleheads, and every third-party aggregator checked — confirmed:true. This is the ONLY currently-active outdoor city pickleball venue in Los Altos; an earlier 2021-2023 pilot also ran at Marymeade, Montclaire, and Rosita parks, but the city's own current FAQ states those three 'remain unchanged' (tennis-only) under the program modification that took effect May 1, 2023, which is when McKenzie was expanded from 4 to 6 courts. Neighborhood ('Loyola Corners') sourced from Foursquare and a local real-estate history page, not from Pickleheads or the city site directly — flagged as lower-confidence. lat/lon geocoded via a third-party tennis-court directory's embedded OpenStreetMap coordinate for this exact address (not a Google-confirmed pin), hence approxLocation:true. Could not confirm a numeric Google Maps cid for this venue after several attempts (short-link search, direct Maps place-URL fetch, geocoding directories) — left null rather than falling back to a place_id or /maps/place/...data= URL. Reservation fee figures ($10/hr resident, $8/hr nonprofit) come from the general 'Tennis Courts' city page and may apply mainly/only to tennis bookings outside pickleball's dedicated priority windows; flagged for human review rather than asserted as pickleball's actual reservation price.

venues.name: Los Altos Golf and Country Club
venues.address: 1560 Country Club Dr, Los Altos, CA 94024
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court/concrete, dedicated permanent lines and nets
venues.price: Membership required (private club; invitation/sponsorship by 4+ current Proprietary members, application process, and initiation fee — specific pickleball court fee not separately published)
venues.hours: Lit for member play until 10pm, 7 days/week (per the club's own site). A separate third-party aggregator instead states Mon-Fri 9am-5pm and Sat-Sun 12pm-8pm — the two are not reconciled; treat the club's own 'up to 10pm' claim as more authoritative.
venues.skill: All ages and skill levels welcome (per club); club hosts drop-in pickleball clinics and social pickleball gatherings for members
venues.weather: Mid-Peninsula foothill town at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains — milder and more sheltered than inland San Jose/Santa Clara, with warm dry summers and mild wet winters; well suited to year-round outdoor play.
venues.notes: Court count (4) independently corroborated by two strong sources: Pickleheads and the club's own official site ('LAGCC has also added four pickleball courts... lit with state of the art LED lights so members can play up to 10pm seven days a week') — confirmed:true. One third-party aggregator (tennispickleballcalifornia.com) states 5 courts and different hours (Mon-Fri 9am-5pm, Sat-Sun 12pm-8pm); treated as a likely error/stale scrape given the club's own site is unambiguous about 4, but flagged here for human review. Amenity list (showers, locker rooms, pro shop, equipment rental, ball machine) sourced only from tennispickleballcalifornia.com, not independently corroborated elsewhere — 'Paid Street Parking' was also listed by that source but excluded here as implausible for a private country club with its own grounds, likely a templated/generic detail rather than venue-specific fact. This is a private, invitation/sponsorship-only club, included per this site's standard of describing membership venues accurately rather than omitting them. googleMapsUrl cid (4879611106093232326) derived by converting the hex feature id (0x43b7dc729cbcf0c6) from the Google Maps place data URL — resolved via a goo.gl short link published on the club's own contacts page — to decimal; lat/lon (37.350987, -122.091781) taken from the same resolved Google Maps URL, so treated as an exact pin (approxLocation:false).

venues.name: Egan Junior High School
venues.address: 100 W Portola Ave, Los Altos, CA 94022
venues.surface: Pickleheads states 8 total wood courts (4 outdoor + 4 indoor) with permanent lines. A separate aggregator instead describes a materially different structure: 2 indoor pickleball courts on a hard/acrylic gym floor plus 4 outdoor courts with portable nets at basketball courts. The city's own official page covers only an indoor gym program and states no court count or surface at all. These are NOT reconciled — see notes.
venues.netType: Bring your own net (per Pickleheads); no dedicated net infrastructure confirmed by the official city source
venues.price: Membership required — annual Los Altos Rec membership. City site states $96 resident/$115 non-resident per year (unlimited drop-in play), with a 1-month free trial; Pickleheads separately states a slightly different non-resident figure ($123) and an age-tiered structure (7-49 yrs vs 50+). Drop-in (non-member) play ended June 1, 2024.
venues.hours: Indoor sessions Mon & Wed, 6:30pm-9:30pm, closed holidays (per official city site, corroborated by Pickleheads and DailyPB). No separate hours given anywhere for the outdoor component Pickleheads describes.
venues.skill: All ages and skill levels welcome (per one aggregator; not stated by the official city source)
venues.weather: Mid-Peninsula foothill town, mild climate overall; the confirmed indoor Egan Gym sessions are weather-independent regardless of season. See surface/notes for the unconfirmed outdoor component Pickleheads describes.
venues.notes: GENUINE UNRESOLVED CONFLICT, left confirmed:false pending human review. The city's own official page (the primary source for this project) describes ONLY an indoor gym pickleball program at Egan Gym -- membership-gated, Mon/Wed 6:30-9:30pm -- and never mentions any outdoor courts at this campus. Pickleheads, however, states 8 total courts split 4 outdoor + 4 indoor (and this 4+4=8 figure is used consistently in both Pickleheads' venue page and its city-level total of 3 locations/18 courts, i.e. 6 McKenzie + 4 LAGCC + 8 Egan = 18, so it is a deliberate stated figure, not a scraping artifact). A third aggregator (tennispickleballcalifornia.com) gives yet another structure: 2 indoor + 4 outdoor = 6 total, though its other details (fees, hours, trial period) match the official city source closely, lending it some credibility on the indoor count specifically. A fourth (globalpickleball.network) says 4 total, all outdoor, with NO fee -- which flatly contradicts the membership requirement every other source agrees on, so it is likely unreliable. A fifth (pickleballrush.com) says 4 total, all indoor, but is explicitly dated 'Last updated: Dec 21, 2018' -- years before the current membership program (est. 2024) existed, so treated as stale/superseded. Recorded courts:8 and indoorOutdoor:'Both' here to match Pickleheads' fully self-consistent figure (also keeping this file's per-venue court counts sum to Pickleheads' own stated 18-court total), while flagging in full that the official primary source does not corroborate any outdoor component at this campus at all. Given this is an active school campus, public access to any outdoor courts here (if they exist) is plausibly restricted outside school-sanctioned hours, unlike a city park -- another reason for human review before treating the outdoor half as a confirmed public amenity. Amenities sourced only from tennispickleballcalifornia.com (single source, not independently corroborated); that source also listed 'shade and covered areas' which was excluded here as implausible for an indoor gym and likely a templated/generic detail. Non-resident membership fee also conflicts slightly ($115 per city site vs $123 per Pickleheads) -- noted in price field rather than resolved. lat/lon geocoded from a school-directory site's embedded driving-directions coordinate for this address, not a Google-confirmed pin, hence approxLocation:true. Could not confirm a numeric Google Maps cid despite attempts.
